paper_title: Triple Oxygen Isotope Measurements (Δ'17O) of Body Water Reflect Water Intake, Metabolism, and δ18O of Ingested Water in Passerines

methods.description: Procedure for measuring triple oxygen isotopes (Δ'17O) from blood plasma samples using cryogenic distillation, chemical reaction, gas purification, and isotope ratio mass spectrometry. This method is used to analyze the isotopic composition of body water.

steps.description: we cryogenically distilled water from 1 to 2 μl blood plasma samples in a vacuum line
steps.searchable_summary: perform cryogenic distillation of water from blood plasma samples

steps.description: then reacted it with BrF 5 at ~300°C for 5-10 min, quantitatively converting H 2 O to O 2 and other gasses.
steps.searchable_summary: react distilled water with BrF5 at high temperature to convert H2O to O2

steps.description: These other gasses were removed via liquid nitrogen traps
steps.searchable_summary: remove unwanted gasses using liquid nitrogen traps

steps.description: and the O 2 was further purified by passing it through a zeolite molecular sieve and a gas chromatography column.
steps.searchable_summary: purify O2 gas using zeolite molecular sieve and gas chromatography

steps.description: O 2 was then analyzed on a dual-inlet Thermo Scientific 253 isotope ratio mass spectrometer (Bremen, Germany) at the University of New Mexico Center for Stable Isotopes (Albuquerque, NM).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze purified O2 gas using isotope ratio mass spectrometry

steps.description: The measured values of δ 17 O and δ 18 O were used to calculate Δ' 17 O (Sharp et al., 2018;Whiteman et al., 2019).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Delta prime 17O from measured delta 17O and delta 18O values

steps.description: At the beginning of each analytical session, we measured a local water standard (NM2: δ 18 O = −13.1‰, δ 17 O = −6.919‰) that had been calibrated against the international water standards VSMOW2 (δ 17 O = δ 18 O = 0.000‰) and SLAP2 (δ 18 O = −55.5‰, δ 17 O = −29.699‰; Schoenemann et al., 2013;Sharp et al., 2016
steps.searchable_summary: measure local and international water standards for calibration and quality control

steps.description: Here, we assumed δ 18 O Air was 19.4‰ because of fractionation that occurs during absorption of inhaled atmospheric oxygen.
steps.searchable_summary: set assumed value for delta 18O of atmospheric oxygen based on fractionation

steps.description: Although such data are lacking for our study species, applying the plausible range of fractionation values for absorbed oxygen (2-6‰) to equation 3 indicates that the resulting estimate of δ 18 O of ingested water generally changes by < 3‰, which is smaller than much of the naturally occurring variation in δ 18 O of potential water sources.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate the impact of assumed oxygen fractionation range on estimated delta 18O of ingested water using equation 3
outputs: estimated change in δ18O of ingested water
